mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when UNRATE remains above 9.0% for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months where INDPRO rises, invalidating the short-term momentum link.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0 while the federal funds rate remains near zero, real interest rates turn deeply negative, triggering inflation hedging flows that depress equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when CPIAUCSL > 215.0, or the threshold effect disappears in a multivariate regression controlling for term premiums.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlates with expanding economic activity and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between payroll growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05, or positive payroll surprises consistently coincide with negative equity returns over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms sustained aggregate demand recovery, which supports corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails when GDP growth is driven by inventory accumulation rather than final sales, resulting in negative 60-day returns despite positive GDPC1 readings, or the signal IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a synchronized supply-labor recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when industrial production rises due to temporary inventory restocking while unemployment remains flat, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample validation.
